articles.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
articles.summary: The Security Service of Ukraine (SSU) said it, together with the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), uncovered a long-running campaign orchestrated by Russian intelligence services to break into the messaging accounts of government officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists in Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S. The systematic cyber attacks aimed at stealing sensitive

articles.title: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
articles.summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

articles.title: CISA Adds Exploited PTC Windchill RCE Flaw to KEV as Web Shell Attacks Continue
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a critical remote code execution vulnerability impacting PTC Windchill PDMlink and PTC FlexPLM enterprise Product Data Management (PDM) and Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) software to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability in question is

articles.title: New SharkLoader Malware Deploys Cobalt Strike in StrikeShark Cyberattacks
articles.summary: A newly discovered cyber attack campaign has been observed delivering a previously undocumented malware family called SharkLoader that acts as a loader for deploying Cobalt Strike Beacon on compromised hosts. Kaspersky, which is tracking the activity under the moniker StrikeShark, said the campaign has targeted a diplomatic organization in Indonesia, government organizations in Taiwan,

articles.title: Miasma Malware Targets npm Packages and GitHub Actions in Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged yet another evolution of the supply chain attack linked to the Mini Shai-Hulud, Miasma, and Hades malware family that has compromised a new set of npm packages, even as it has propagated to the Go ecosystem. "The latest activity includes malicious npm releases affecting LeoPlatform and RStreams packages, GitHub Actions workflow abuse, and a related Go

articles.title: Uganda's leading media outlets shut down by army chief
articles.summary: NTV and Daily Monitor say their offices in the capital are under "military siege".

articles.title: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
articles.summary: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.

articles.title: Europe's deadly heatwave breaks German record and halts public events
articles.summary: Germany's highest ever temperature of 41.3C is recorded provisionally in Saarbrücken, over the border from France.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Violence displaces thousands in Haiti and Lebanon, Gaza updates, UN food agency delivers in Ebola-stricken DR Congo
articles.summary: More than 2,600 people were displaced in the Artibonite department of Haiti following clashes between armed groups last week as the humanitarian situation continues to worsen, according to the UN’s Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Assistance (OCHA).

articles.title: Heatwave breaks records in Germany, Denmark and Czech Republic
articles.summary: An estimated 150 million people have been experiencing temperatures of over 35C across Europe.

articles.title: Trump's face is added to select US passports for America's 250th birthday
articles.summary: The commemorative special edition passports will be available on 6 July, and can only be applied for in person

articles.title: Could legal 'personhood' help save Lough Neagh?
articles.summary: Rights of Nature could give Lough Neagh the same status as a person, entitled to the same protections.

articles.title: NASA’s PACE Mission Studies Smoke, Fires
articles.summary: With the North American fire season underway, and a record number of acres already burned nationwide, NASA’s Plankton, Aerosol, Cloud, and ocean Ecosystem (PACE) satellite’s three instruments are observing vegetation precursors to fires, along with plumes of smoke and their movement. This data will help scientists piece together clues that deepen their understanding of wildfires. […]

articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Heat waves mess with your brain. articles.title: Heat waves mess with your brain. Scientists are trying to figure out why.
articles.summary: It’s been hot in London this week. Really hot. A dangerous heat wave has hit Western Europe. Yesterday, the UK recorded its highest ever June temperature at 36.1 °C (about 97 °F). But as the weather app on my phone confirmed, it felt like 39 °C. It’s frightening that we are seeing such temperatures in…

articles.title: The Download: Europe’s heat wave hits the grid, and IBM’s chip targets Moore’s Law
articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Europe’s extreme heat is shutting down power plants Europe is in the middle of a record-breaking heat wave, and the grid is being pushed to its limits as people turn to…

articles.title: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 Sol With Restricted Access and Stronger Cyber Safeguards
articles.summary: OpenAI on Friday released three versions of GPT-5.6, called Sol, Terra, and Luna, as a limited preview to a small number of companies as part of an ongoing engagement with the U.S. government. While Sol is the latest flagship model and the most powerful, Terra strikes a balance between efficiency and power, and Luna is fine-tuned for speed and affordability. "GPT‑5.6 Sol launches with our most

articles.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys
articles.summary: The FBI and CISA have updated their March warning about Russian intelligence phishing Signal accounts, and the operators have added a step: they now coax targets into handing over their Signal Backup Recovery Key. Hand it over once, and the attacker can restore the account's backup, read the private and group message history, and take over the account. Worse, the key keeps working.

articles.title: Chinese-Speaking APT Deploys New TinyRCT Backdoor in Southeast Asia Campaign
articles.summary: A Chinese-speaking advanced persistent threat (APT) actor has been linked to a new custom backdoor called TinyRCT as part of cyber attacks aimed at government entities and critical infrastructure in Southeast Asia. The activity, particularly aimed at state-owned enterprises in the energy and government sectors, has been attributed to a threat actor called CL-STA-1062, which Palo Alto Networks

articles.title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code via MCP Configs
articles.summary: A high-severity flaw in Amazon Q Developer let a malicious repository run commands and steal a developer's cloud credentials. The path was short: a developer opens the repo, trusts the workspace, and Amazon Q does the rest. Amazon has patched it. Tracked as CVE-2026-12957 (CVSS 8.5), the bug sat in how Amazon's AI coding assistant handled Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Wiz

articles.title: New DirtyClone Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local Users Gain Root via Cloned Packets
articles.summary: DirtyClone is a new Linux kernel privilege escalation in the DirtyFrag family. JFrog Security Research published a working exploit walkthrough for the flaw on June 25, the first public demonstration for this variant. Tracked as CVE-2026-43503 (CVSS 8.8), it lets a local user corrupt file-backed memory through a cloned network packet and gain root. The patch landed in

articles.title: French court rules Total must revise climate plan to account for all emissions
articles.summary: Oil and gas giant TotalEnergies has been given six months by a court to publish a more robust climate plan The post French court rules Total must revise climate plan to account for all emissions appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: UN asks AI companies to reveal full environmental impacts
articles.summary: UN chief António Guterres says big AI firms should disclose the emissions, water and energy use of their data centres amid community opposition The post UN asks AI companies to reveal full environmental impacts appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Two boys rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble after days of being trapped
articles.summary: Rescuers spent six hours carefully digging to reach one of the boys, as many residents continue searching through the rubble by hand, still waiting for heavy machinery to arrive.

articles.title: Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France
articles.summary: The pilot and 10 passengers died in the incident, local officials said.

articles.title: Australia to double maximum penalty for platforms in breach of social media ban
articles.summary: Children under the age of 16 have been prevented from 10 key social media platforms in Australia since December 2025.

articles.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
articles.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

articles.title: I hear my son crying beneath the rubble, says Venezuela earthquake survivor
articles.summary: Andreina Valerio says she fears for her son, who is trapped with her partner and in-laws beneath a collapsed building.

articles.title: Are we in for a summer of serial heatwaves?
articles.summary: Forecasters have suggested that summer is likely to be warmer than average with an increased chance of more heatwaves, as Simon King explains.

articles.title: In pictures: Britain swelters in record-breaking June heat
articles.summary: A rare red weather warning is in place as extreme temperatures continue across much of the UK.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz: UN evacuates 2,500 seafarers before attack freezes rescue operation
articles.summary: The United Nations’ maritime agency said on Friday that it had successfully evacuated about 2,500 stranded seafarers from the Persian Gulf before suspending the operation, after an attack on a commercial vessel exposed uncertainty over who can guarantee safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Ebola prevention, Yemen child deaths, Colombia elections, Japan climate campaign
articles.summary: The UN's top humanitarian official has allocated $8 million in funding to help Burundi and South Sudan prepare for the potential spread of Ebola.

articles.title: NASA Tests New Refuel Device for Future In-Space Refueling Missions
articles.summary: For NASA’s next generation of deep space exploration missions, spacecraft may need to refuel in Earth orbit before pushing farther into the solar system. Similar to how a gas pump needs a nozzle to fit your fuel tank, future spacecraft could require a special device in order to fill up prior to departure, known as […]

articles.title: Euclid Sees Heart of Milky Way
articles.summary: Euclid, an ESA (European Space Agency) mission with NASA contributions, took a new look at the heart of our Milky Way galaxy, seen in this image released on June 24, 2026. This observation overlaps with a region scientists will observe with NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, launching later this summer. This sneak peek gives […]

articles.title: IBM has unveiled chip technology that could help extend Moore’s Law another decade
articles.summary: IBM has built a new prototype chip with around 100 billion transistors on an area the size of a fingernail, which is twice the density of the company’s previous state-of-the-art technology announced in 2021. The design could pave the way for faster and more energy efficient computers for years to come. For more than half…

articles.title: Microsoft Warns of Photo ZIP Phishing Campaign Targeting Hotels with Node.js Implant
articles.summary: An active phishing campaign has been targeting hotel and other hospitality organizations across Europe and Asia since April 2026, using photo-themed ZIP files to drop a Node.js implant and dig into front-desk machines, Microsoft says. The company has not attributed the activity to a known threat actor, and the operators' end goal is still unclear. The lure plays to how hotels work.

articles.title: UN chief says fossil fuel industry must cut methane for warming “relief”
articles.summary: The UN Secretary-General urges drastic reductions in methane emissions, starting with the energy sector, to limit an expected overshoot of global warming beyond 1.5C The post UN chief says fossil fuel industry must cut methane for warming “relief” appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: China’s coal-chemicals boom risks repeating the mistakes of the past
articles.summary: By reviving the problematic industry in the name of energy security, China runs the risk of higher emissions, stranded assets and setting back its ambitions as a clean energy leader The post China’s coal-chemicals boom risks repeating the mistakes of the past appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history
articles.summary: Rescue teams are working ceaselessly to reach those trapped under rubble. But as hope fades, anger is growing.

articles.title: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
articles.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

articles.title: 117 dead dogs found at California 'no-kill' animal rescue - many with gunshot wounds
articles.summary: Hundreds more animals are still missing from the California facility as investigators continue excavating the grounds.
